title: Validation Case 2 — adaptability: change-surface to swap a RAG pipeline's vector store (in-memory → lance)
thesis: When a requirement changes (swap the RAG pipeline's vector store from in-memory to a persistent backend), the spec-driven CrewHaus path is a one-line backend-selector edit (plus one optional on-disk-index-path line) + a deterministic recompile that re-wires the emitted bundle; the hand-built LangGraph path is a real, multi-line rewrite — a new connection + table lifecycle, row serialise/reconstruct, a dependency, and a sync→async cascade across every store call-site.

provenance.enabledBy: factory PR #182 (commit 513a961, merged to factory main 2026-06-04) — `feat: enable vector-store backend selection (lance/qdrant/pinecone/weaviate)`. PR #182 widened both the IR selector and the spec enum from the literal "in-memory" to the full implemented backend set, making the vector-store swap spec-drivable for the first time.
provenance.vectorStoreFactory: @crewhaus/vector-store (packages/vector-store/src/index.ts) — createVectorStore({ backend, url?, collection?, apiKey? }); backends: in-memory | lance | qdrant | pinecone | weaviate. The lance backend (packages/vector-store/src/backends/lance.ts) is file-backed: NDJSON on disk by default, and lights up the real @lancedb/lancedb SDK when that dependency is present.
provenance.lanceClient: @lancedb/lancedb@0.30.0 — the real LanceDB client, used directly by the hand-built copy.
provenance.measurementMethod: git diff --numstat + plain `diff` over the original artifact vs an edited COPY; the originals are left untouched on this branch.

scenarioChosen.name: vector-store backend swap (in-memory → lance)
scenarioChosen.previouslyBlocked: BEFORE factory #182 this swap was NOT spec-drivable: IrPipelineV0.retrieve.vectorBackend was the literal "in-memory" and the spec mirrored it with z.enum(["in-memory"]). A spec-only change had no effect, even though all alternative backends were already implemented in @crewhaus/vector-store. The earlier draft of this case therefore (a) recorded the swap as 'not supported / blocked by type narrowness' and (b) measured an EMBEDDER swap as a stand-in.
scenarioChosen.nowEnabled: factory #182 widened the IR selector to IrVectorBackend = "in-memory" | "lance" | "qdrant" | "pinecone" | "weaviate" (packages/ir/src/index.ts:574) and the spec to vectorBackend: z.enum(VECTOR_BACKENDS).default("in-memory") over VECTOR_BACKENDS = ["in-memory", "lance", "qdrant", "pinecone", "weaviate"] (packages/spec/src/index.ts:600,630). The compiler's lower() passes vectorBackend (and optional url/collection/apiKey) through to the IR unchanged (packages/compiler/src/index.ts:739,741-743) and the emitter bakes them into createVectorStore({ ... }) (packages/target-pipeline/src/index.ts:57-63). So the swap is now exactly the one-line procedure below — ZERO compiler changes.
scenarioChosen.lanceConfigRequirement: For the lance backend, `vectorBackend: lance` alone is sufficient — the spec parser only requires url+collection for the HTTP backends (qdrant/pinecone/weaviate; parseSpec packages/spec/src/index.ts:1137), and the factory defaults the lance index path to ".crewhaus/vectors/lance" when no url is given. We additionally set retrieve.url to an explicit on-disk index path (the IR documents lance's `url` as 'the on-disk index path', ir/src/index.ts:588-593) so the benchmark writes to a deterministic, known location. That makes the CrewHaus edit one CHANGED line (the selector) + one ADDED line (the optional on-disk path).
scenarioChosen.embedderSwapIsSecondary: The same one-line procedure applies to other backend-selecting fields; the embedder swap (retrieve.embedderModel: mock/det → openai/text-embedding-3-small) is measured separately as a secondary confirmation (see embedderSwapSecondary below).

addedLine.note: optional explicit on-disk lance index path; lance does not require it (factory defaults it), but we set it for a deterministic index location

specChange.developerTouchedLines: 1 changed selector line + 1 added optional config line

recompile.deterministicBaselineCheck: A fresh recompile of the UNMODIFIED in-memory spec byte-matched the committed crewhaus-rag/dist/agent.ts (after stripping the generated header comment), confirming the compiler is deterministic before measuring the diff.

bundleDiff.note: Exactly one line of the emitted bundle changed — the createVectorStore() wiring. Everything else (pipeline graph, embedder, retrieve config, runtime wiring) is byte-identical. The lance backend (+ on-disk path) is genuinely baked into the recompiled bundle by the real factory compiler.

dependencyAdded.note: A hand-built engineer adopting Lance must add the client dependency. CrewHaus already ships @crewhaus/vector-store (with the lance backend) behind the one-line vectorBackend field — no new dependency on the user's side.

whatChanged: Replaced the inlined synchronous InMemoryVectorStore (a process Map with cosine top-k) with a LanceVectorStore class backed by the real @lancedb/lancedb client (on-disk index).

Added a connection + table lifecycle the in-memory store never had: a one-time async init() that opens lancedb.connect(dir), and lazy table creation on first write (createTable defines the schema; subsequent writes append via table.add).

Serialised each Chunk into flat Lance columns (id/docId/text/vector) on upsert, and reconstructed the Chunk shape on query, translating Lance's L2 `_distance` back into the score shape the graph nodes already consume.
Dropped the hand-rolled cosine() helper (Lance computes distance natively).

Made every store operation async (upsert/count/search now return Promises), which cascaded into the graph: indexNode now awaits getStore() + awaits each upsert + awaits count; retrieveNode now awaits getStore() + awaits search. The module-scope `const store = new InMemoryVectorStore()` became a lazily-initialised memoised getStore().

changeSurface.method: git diff --numstat over original vs edited copy (file content only; the dependency add in package.json/bun.lock is recorded separately above)
changeSurface.newAsyncLifecycle: store init/connect + lazy table create (did not exist in the sync in-memory store)
changeSurface.codeOnlyDefinition: excludes blank lines and comment-only lines (//, /*, *, */)

validity.typecheck: Bun-style typecheck (tsc --noEmit --allowImportingTsExtensions --module esnext --moduleResolution bundler --target esnext --types bun --skipLibCheck) on the edited copy reports exactly ONE error: TS2339 'reason' on ModelResult at line 285.
validity.errorEvidence: The identical TS2339 'reason' error exists in the UNTOUCHED original langgraph-rag.ts at line 232 (in generateNode's offline fallback — `${result.reason}` — code NOT touched by this swap). The lance store swap therefore introduces ZERO new type errors — it is a real, valid, runnable implementation, not a stub.
validity.liveRun: The edited copy runs end-to-end against the real @lancedb/lancedb client AND the live Anthropic model (claude-sonnet-4-6): indexed 4 chunks into an on-disk Lance index, Lance vector search returned 4 ranked hits (section-19#0 top for 'what target shapes exist?'), and the model returned a grounded answer citing [1][2][3]. (4 chunks here vs 5 on the CrewHaus side is a pre-existing chunker difference between the hand-built corpus and the YAML corpus, unrelated to the store swap.)

metric_changeSurface.definition: Developer-authored lines a human must write/rewrite to effect the vector-store swap.
metric_changeSurface.ratio_touched: 103:1 (vs the single changed selector line) / ~52:1 (vs the 2-line CrewHaus edit)
metric_changeSurface.ratio_codeOnly: 73:1 (vs the single changed selector line) / ~37:1 (vs the 2-line CrewHaus edit)
metric_changeSurface.headline: CrewHaus: 1 changed selector line (+1 optional on-disk-path line) + recompile re-wires 1 bundle line, runs live. Hand-built: 103 lines touched (73 code-only) + a new dependency, including a new connection/table lifecycle, row serialise/reconstruct, and a sync→async cascade across 3 call-sites.

crewhausLanceBundle_endToEnd.command: printf 'what target shapes exist?\n' | bun benchmarks/langgraph-vs-crewhaus/crewhaus-rag-lance/dist/agent.ts   (with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY exported from .env)
crewhausLanceBundle_endToEnd.observed: Indexing pipeline ran end-to-end into the LANCE store (chunk → embed → store → 'indexed 5 chunks'); the on-disk lance index .crewhaus/vectors/bench-rag-lance/default.jsonl was written (6905 bytes, mode 0600) containing the embedded vectors as NDJSON; the Retrieve tool fired against the lance store; the live Anthropic model claude-sonnet-4-6 returned a grounded answer citing retrieved chunks [2][3][4].
crewhausLanceBundle_endToEnd.conclusion: The recompiled CrewHaus pipeline bundle genuinely uses the file-backed lance backend and works end-to-end against the live model API.

handBuiltLance_endToEnd.command: bun benchmarks/langgraph-vs-crewhaus/langgraph-rag-lance.ts "what target shapes exist?"   (with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY exported from .env)
handBuiltLance_endToEnd.observed: Used the REAL @lancedb/lancedb client: indexed 4 chunks into an on-disk Lance database, Lance vector search returned 4 ranked hits, and the live Anthropic model returned a grounded answer citing [1][2][3].
handBuiltLance_endToEnd.conclusion: The hand-built lance swap is a real, runnable implementation against the genuine LanceDB SDK — not a stub.

liveSanity.limitations: All runs above used the deterministic mock embedder (mock/det) so retrieval is reproducible offline; the only network call is the Anthropic model. No OPENAI_API_KEY is present in this environment (demos/.env holds only ANTHROPIC_API_KEY), which is irrelevant to the vector-store swap (the embedder is unchanged here).

embedderSwapSecondary.note: Secondary confirmation that the same one-line backend-selector procedure applies to other fields. Measured separately; the vector store is the headline Case 2.
embedderSwapSecondary.wiringProof: OPENAI_API_KEY= bun crewhaus-rag-openai/dist/agent.ts "q" → EmbedderError: openai embedder requires OPENAI_API_KEY (thrown from createEmbedder's case "openai" branch at the recompiled dist/agent.ts:17) — proving the recompiled bundle routes into the real OpenAI provider path. A full OpenAI network run is not possible here (no OPENAI_API_KEY).

reproduction: # --- CrewHaus side: 1-line selector swap (+1 optional on-disk path) + real recompile ---
# edit retrieve: change `vectorBackend: in-memory` -> `vectorBackend: lance`; add `url: .crewhaus/vectors/bench-rag-lance`
# --- Hand-built side: faithful real-LanceDB store swap on a copy ---
# replace the inlined InMemoryVectorStore with a @lancedb/lancedb-backed LanceVectorStore (connect + table + add + search); make the store async + memoised; await it in indexNode and retrieveNode
# --- Live sanity (deterministic mock embedder; live Anthropic model) ---
printf 'what target shapes exist?\n' | bun benchmarks/langgraph-vs-crewhaus/crewhaus-rag-lance/dist/agent.ts   # CrewHaus: indexes into lance NDJSON, retrieves, real answer, exit 0
bun benchmarks/langgraph-vs-crewhaus/langgraph-rag-lance.ts "what target shapes exist?"   # hand-built: real @lancedb/lancedb index + search + real answer, exit 0
